marketContext.conflictAlert: CEASEFIRE SIGNALS — Middle East pivot in progress

conflictInsights.label: Defense backlogs are irrevocable
conflictInsights.desc: Multi-year RTX, LMT, NOC contracts signed during the conflict cannot be cancelled — backlog strength persists regardless of ceasefire outcome

conflictInsights.label: Oil direction is the key variable
conflictInsights.desc: WTI pulled back from $118 to ~$91 on ceasefire signals — a confirmed deal pushes oil to $70–75; a collapse returns it to $110+. Position sizing in energy should reflect this binary

conflictInsights.label: Gold floor established
conflictInsights.desc: Central banks accelerating gold purchases as geopolitical norms shift — structural buyer base independent of any single conflict outcome

conflictInsights.label: NATO mandate is permanent
conflictInsights.desc: European 2% GDP defense commitment is now treaty-level — defense supercycle is structural, not tactical, and survives any Middle East resolution

themes.label: Geopolitical pivot
themes.desc: Iran ceasefire signals de-escalating conflict — oil pulling back from $118 peak, but defense backlogs and gold floor are permanent shifts

themes.label: Defense supercycle
themes.desc: NATO 2% GDP mandate + locked-in multi-year backlogs make RTX a structural compounder independent of any ceasefire

themes.label: Gold at all-time highs
themes.desc: ~$3,310/oz — central bank buying + geopolitical uncertainty providing a structural floor well above historical norms

themes.label: AI capex intact
themes.desc: NVDA, META, AMZN, GOOG reported strong Q1 — enterprise AI infrastructure spend is disconnected from geopolitical headlines

themes.label: Healthcare secular
themes.desc: GLP-1 + aging demographics = multi-decade demand runway for NVO, ISRG, LLY regardless of macro cycle

themes.label: Fed frozen
themes.desc: Fed held for 3rd meeting — 8-4 hawkish split; near-zero chance of cuts in 2026 as CPI stays sticky at 3.3%. Rate-cut thesis is on hold

themes.label: TSX resilience
themes.desc: Canadian equities outperforming on energy + financials strength; BoC on hold at 2.25% but domestic economy more insulated than US

themes.label: Oil uncertainty
themes.desc: Ceasefire scenario = oil to $70–75 (bearish for energy names); collapse scenario = back to $110+ (bullish). Position sizing matters more than direction

recommendations.name: Berkshire Hathaway B
recommendations.thesis: Sitting on $330B+ cash amid tariff volatility — Buffett deploys aggressively in corrections. Natural market hedge with zero dividend drag. Complements tech-heavy TFSA with value ballast. The ultimate shock absorber: it gets more valuable in downturns.
risks: Succession uncertainty post-Buffett
Underperforms growth in prolonged bull markets
Insurance exposure to catastrophic weather events

recommendations.name: Enbridge
recommendations.thesis: Largest North American pipeline — 30% of US crude flows through Enbridge's network. Revenue is set by the National Energy Board regardless of spot oil price: the safest energy income play in any environment. 6.8% dividend with 0% WHT in TFSA. 29 consecutive years of dividend growth. Own for income, not oil direction.
risks: Line 5 Michigan legal challenge — potential forced shutdown
Rate regulation caps revenue growth upside
High debt load (D/E > 1.5x) — rate-sensitive balance sheet

recommendations.name: Costco Wholesale
recommendations.thesis: Membership model is recession-proof — members don't cancel during downturns, they shop more. International expansion accelerating. Tariff pressure minimal as private-label pricing power offsets costs. One of the few retailers that benefits from consumer stress.
risks: Trades at 50x+ P/E — expensive for a retailer
Slowing international store openings vs expectations
Tariff pass-through compressing gross margins

recommendations.name: Visa
recommendations.thesis: Digital payments toll-road untouched by tariffs. Global cross-border volume growing as travel rebounds. Asset-light royalty model: $0.001 per transaction × billions of transactions = immense free cash flow. One of the widest moats in public markets.
risks: Regulatory interchange fee caps (EU, US)
Crypto payment rails as long-term substitute
DOJ antitrust scrutiny of card network duopoly

recommendations.name: Intuitive Surgical
recommendations.thesis: 7,000+ da Vinci robotic systems installed creates razor/blade recurring revenue. New Ion bronchoscopy expanding TAM. Secular shift to minimally invasive surgery = 20-year compounding runway. Hospitals don't switch once trained on a platform.
risks: Premium valuation (55x+ P/E)
J&J Ottava robotic system entering market
Procedure volume tied to elective surgery cycles

recommendations.name: Novo Nordisk
recommendations.thesis: GLP-1 peer to LLY at a cheaper valuation. Ozempic/Wegovy demand still exceeds supply. Oral semaglutide + next-gen CagriSema data due in 2026. Portfolio: LLY + NVO = dual GLP-1 coverage across two IP estates.
risks: CagriSema Phase 3 trial miss risk
LLY oral tirzepatide as direct competitor
US drug pricing reform compressing GLP-1 margins

recommendations.name: ARM Holdings
recommendations.thesis: CPU architecture royalty on every advanced chip. Powers all Apple silicon, Nvidia data center CPUs, and AI edge devices. Scales to $100B revenue with zero manufacturing risk — the ultimate AI leverage play without semiconductor capex risk.
risks: RISC-V open-source architecture gaining traction
Customer concentration (Apple, Qualcomm, Nvidia)
SoftBank overhang on share price

recommendations.name: Axon Enterprise
recommendations.thesis: AI law enforcement platform (Taser + cameras + Evidence.com SaaS). Government contracts = recession-proof revenue. International expansion accelerating. Zero dividend = pure TFSA compounder. Agencies that adopt Axon rarely leave — hardware, software and evidence management are all locked in together.
risks: Rich valuation (80x+ P/E)
Political / regulatory backlash on police surveillance AI
Government contract concentration risk

recommendations.name: Shopify
recommendations.thesis: Canadian fintech/commerce platform powering 10%+ of US e-commerce. AI tools (Sidekick) driving merchant GMV growth. Freed from logistics. Expanding into B2B commerce globally. Zero dividend = pure tax-free TFSA growth. Only Canadian tech name in the portfolio.
risks: Growth premium valuation — punished severely on any miss
Amazon and TikTok Shop competing for same merchants
Merchant GMV exposed to consumer spending slowdown

recommendations.name: RTX Corporation
recommendations.thesis: The most diversified defense compounder: Patriot missile systems, Raytheon munitions, Pratt & Whitney F-35 engines, and commercial aero MRO. Backlog extends past 2031 — ceasefire signals don't cancel a single unit. NATO 2% GDP commitment is now treaty-level, making RTX a decade-long structural compounder.
risks: GTF engine recall costs still unresolved
Cost-plus contract margin pressure from inflation
Ceasefire could slow near-term order intake optically

recommendations.name: Suncor Energy
recommendations.thesis: Suncor's ~$45/bbl break-even is the lowest of any major oil sands producer — profitable at $65 oil. 25%+ share buyback since 2020. CAD-listed = 0% WHT in TFSA. Size this position to your conviction on oil staying above $75. The energy cyclical with the most downside protection.
risks: WTI to $70–75 on ceasefire squeezes margins materially
Oil sands carbon tax escalation in Canada
Pipeline capacity constraints limit export optionality

recommendations.name: iShares MSCI India ETF
recommendations.thesis: India = China replacement manufacturing hub. Apple, Samsung shifting production. 1.4B population, median age 28, 7%+ GDP growth. Modi infrastructure boom. Demographics + manufacturing shift = generational opportunity. The single best vehicle for India exposure with broad index coverage.
risks: Single-country concentration — political or macro shock hits the entire ETF
INR/CAD currency risk on repatriation
Elevated valuations post-2023 rally (Nifty 50 at 22x P/E)

recommendations.name: Vanguard European ETF
recommendations.thesis: European stocks at 40-year discount to US on P/E. Defense spending surge (NATO 2% GDP mandate). ECB cutting rates faster than Fed. Deep geographic diversification away from US concentration. Broad multi-country diversification means no single European shock wipes the position.
risks: EUR/CAD currency headwind if ECB cuts faster than expected
Russia-Ukraine conflict escalation risk
Slower structural earnings growth vs US tech-heavy market

recommendations.name: iShares 20+ Year Treasury Bond
recommendations.thesis: TLT at 4.35% yield is pure carry while you wait for the Fed pivot. The trade is asymmetric: if oil falls on ceasefire, CPI drops to ~2.8% and the Fed cuts — delivering 15–20% capital gain on top of yield. Best shock absorber when equities sell off hard. Hold in RRSP to shelter interest income.
risks: Duration risk — 20yr bonds fall ~15% per 1% rise in rates
Fed staying higher for longer delays the capital gain payoff
US fiscal deficit puts structural upward pressure on long-end yields

recommendations.name: BMO Aggregate Bond ETF
recommendations.thesis: 3.2% yield with zero currency risk and 0% WHT in TFSA. Portfolio ballast: when equities sell off, ZAG.TO rises. BoC cut cycle will resume when domestic growth softens further (GDP projected 1.2% for 2026) — making this a patient carry trade with defensive optionality. Right-size it as insurance, not a momentum bet.
risks: BoC on hold longer than expected delays capital appreciation
Low total return vs equity alternatives in a risk-on market
Rising Canadian sovereign debt concerns if fiscal deficit widens
